extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: low to moderate (open workbook, export formulas, update memo and re-run precheck)

planned_changes: Open and inspect attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] produce an exported file listing all derived cells and their formulas (sheet, cell, formula). Attach the export as [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] or .txt.
Create a mapping artifact ([EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] or .json) that ties every derived/decision cell to the exact embedded-packet field (sheet name, row id, packet field name).

If possible, regenerate the workbook with an openpyxl/xlsxwriter compatible build or include an exported workbook in an alternate parseable format (e.g., .ods or zipped sheet XML plus formula text) so the evaluation environment can programmatically validate formulas.

Update enclave_release_memo.md: remove definitive language claiming full semantic verification; add a Validation Outcome subsection documenting which checks were automated, which were human-verified, and attach the new formulas export and mapping artifacts as evidence.
Re-run deterministic precheck and submit a revised grader invocation that omits unverified semantic_score assertions until the .xlsx is parsed or inspected.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address unsupported semantic claims by providing machine-readable/spreadsheet evidence and clarifying memo claims so verifier can confirm formulas and row-level citations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts and memo are present and the deterministic precheck passed, but the grader asserted full semantic correctness and absence of fabrication despite the .xlsx being unparsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable).
